34	## Origins
35	
36	### Early life
37	
38	In [172 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest), Aegor was born at [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing) to the fifth of King [Aegon IV Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_IV_Targaryen)'s mistresses, [Barba Bracken](wiki:Barba_Bracken). Aegor was born only a fortnight before Aegon's wife, Queen [Naerys Targaryen](wiki:Naerys_Targaryen), nearly died in childbirth. With Naerys lingering near death, [Barba's father](wiki:Lord_Bracken_(Hand_of_the_King)), the [Hand of the King](wiki:Hand_of_the_King), began to speak openly of Barba marrying King Aegon IV. However, his words became a scandal when Queen Naerys recovered her health. Naerys's brother, Prince [Aemon the Dragonknight](wiki:Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_II)), and her son, Prince [Daeron Targaryen](wiki:Daeron_II_Targaryen), forced King Aegon to send both Barba and her [bastard](wiki:Bastard) son away from court. Aegor was raised at [Stone Hedge](wiki:Stone_Hedge) in the [riverlands](wiki:Riverlands) by the Brackens.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iv.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-2)
39	
40	King Aegon IV was visiting Aegor at Stone Hedge in [177 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest) when he met Barba's younger sister, [Bethany Bracken](wiki:Bethany_Bracken), and took her for his seventh mistress. However, Bethany's affair with Ser [Terrence Toyne](wiki:Terrence_Toyne), a [Kingsguard](wiki:Kingsguard), soon resulted in the execution of Terrence, Bethany, and her father, Aegor's grandfather.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iv.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-3)
41	
42	The angry Aegor reserved particular loathing for his half-brother [Brynden Rivers](wiki:Brynden_Rivers), whose mother, [Melissa Blackwood](wiki:Melissa_Blackwood), had replaced his own as their father's favorite mistress. Aegor loathed Brynden, who was known as Bloodraven, even further when [Shiera Seastar](wiki:Shiera_Seastar), their beautiful half-sister and a fellow [Great Bastard](wiki:Great_Bastard), chose Brynden over him. Their other half-brother, [Daemon Blackfyre](wiki:Daemon_I_Blackfyre), agreed to wed his daughter [Calla](wiki:Calla_Blackfyre) to Aegor; however, it is unknown if and when the marriage took place. Aegor often urged Daemon to press his own claim to the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne).(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_daeron_ii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-5)
43	
44	### First Blackfyre Rebellion
45	
46	In the [First Blackfyre Rebellion](wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion) in [196 AC](wiki:196_AC), Aegor sided with the pretender king [Daemon I Blackfyre](wiki:Daemon_I_Blackfyre) against King [Daeron II Targaryen](wiki:Daeron_II_Targaryen). During the [battle of the Redgrass Field](wiki:Battle_of_the_Redgrass_Field), Aegor commanded the right of Daemon's host.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rtmk.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-0)
47	
48	To prevent a rout upon Daemon's death during the battle, Aegor picked up Daemon's [Valyrian steel](wiki:Valyrian_steel) sword, [Blackfyre](wiki:Blackfyre), rallied his troops, and madly charged into the [Raven's Teeth](wiki:Raven's_Teeth), the archers of Brynden Rivers. Bittersteel met Bloodraven in the middle of his charge, and a mighty duel followed. Though he took out Bloodraven's eye during their duel, Aegor ultimately fled the battlefield.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rtss.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-0)(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_daeron_ii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-6)
49	
50	Bittersteel fled Westeros with Daemon's widow, [Rohanne of Tyrosh](wiki:Rohanne_of_Tyrosh), her surviving children, the blade Blackfyre, and hundreds of now-landless lords and knights, eventually settling in the [Free City](wiki:Free_Cities) of [Tyrosh](wiki:Tyrosh).(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rtss.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-1)(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Radwd5.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-0)
51	
52	### The Golden Company
53	
54	Aegor served a year with the [Second Sons](wiki:Second_Sons) mercenary company.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Radwd66.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-0) However, as other exile lords began to join other mercenary companies and the support for the [Blackfyres](wiki:House_Blackfyre) began to scatter, Bittersteel left the Second Sons to create the [Golden Company](wiki:Golden_Company), to bind the exiles together.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Radwd5.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-1) Shortly after its creation, the Golden Company became one of the most famous and disciplined [sellsword](wiki:Sellsword) companies known in the [Free Cities](wiki:Free_Cities). They quickly established their reputation by sacking [Qohor](wiki:Qohor) when the city refused to honor its contract.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rawoiafaegor_rivers.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-1) While sellswords are notoriously unreliable, the Golden Company has a respectable reputation. They are said to have never broken a contract.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Raffc13.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-0) The war cry of the Golden Company is "Beneath the [gold](wiki:Gold), the bitter [steel](wiki:Steel),"(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Raffc21.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-0)(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Radwd5.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-2) which refers to Aegor's nickname, Bittersteel.
55	
56	The Golden Company was heavily involved in three of the four [Blackfyre Rebellions](wiki:Blackfyre_Pretenders) that followed the [first one](wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion). However, Aegor refused to support the pretender king [Daemon II Blackfyre](wiki:Daemon_II_Blackfyre) during the [Second Blackfyre Rebellion](wiki:Second_Blackfyre_Rebellion) in [211 AC](wiki:211_AC), and [maesters](wiki:Maester) of the [Citadel](wiki:Citadel) often debate why. Many believe that Daemon and Lord [Gormon Peake](wiki:Gormon_Peake) could not convince Aegor that their plan was sensible and could succeed despite the odds. Some think Bittersteel was a man who had little use for anything but war and distrusted Daemon's [dreams](wiki:Dream) and love of fine things, and some think Daemon's homosexuality troubled Aegor enough to deny him his assistance.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aerys_i.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-1) Bittersteel remained in [Essos](wiki:Essos) with the sword [Blackfyre](wiki:Blackfyre), a factor in the failure of the rebellion.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rtmk.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-1)
57	
58	In [219 AC](wiki:219_AC), [Haegon I Blackfyre](wiki:Haegon_I_Blackfyre) and Bittersteel launched the [Third Blackfyre Rebellion](wiki:Third_Blackfyre_Rebellion). During the rebellion, Bloodraven and Bittersteel dueled for the second time. At the end of the battle, Haegon was killed after surrendering, but Aegor was captured and brought to the [Red Keep](wiki:Red_Keep) in chains. Though Brynden Rivers and Prince [Aerion Targaryen](wiki:Aerion_Targaryen) argued that Aegor should be executed, King [Aerys I Targaryen](wiki:Aerys_I_Targaryen) decided to send him to the [Wall](wiki:Wall) to join the [Night's Watch](wiki:Night's_Watch). However, the ship taking Aegor north was intercepted, and he was freed and returned to the Golden Company. Before the year was over, Aegor crowned [Daemon III Blackfyre](wiki:Daemon_III_Blackfyre), Haegon I's eldest son, and resumed plotting against King Aerys in [Tyrosh](wiki:Tyrosh).(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aerys_i.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-2)
59	
60	During one of the Blackfyre Rebellions, Aegor swore a blood oath with [Torwyn Greyjoy](wiki:Torwyn_Greyjoy), but this [Lord Reaper of Pyke](wiki:Lord_Reaper_of_Pyke) betrayed Bittersteel to his enemies.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_iron_islands:_the_old_way_and_the_new.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_15-0)
61	
62	In [236 AC](wiki:236_AC), Aegor landed on [Massey's Hook](wiki:Massey's_Hook) at the head of the Golden Company with Daemon III, starting the [Fourth Blackfyre Rebellion](wiki:Fourth_Blackfyre_Rebellion). The rebellion was crushed at the [Battle of Wendwater Bridge](wiki:Battle_of_Wendwater_Bridge), though Aegor managed to escape.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_v.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-1)(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_westerlands:_house_lannister_under_the_dragons.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_16-0) A few years after that battle, Aegor reappeared in the [Disputed Lands](wiki:Disputed_Lands), fighting with the Golden Company in a skirmish between Tyrosh and [Myr](wiki:Myr).(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_v.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-2)
63	
64	When Aegor died at the age of sixty-nine in [241 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest), it is said he died as he had lived, with a sword in his hand and defiance upon his lips.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_v.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-3) On his deathbed, he commanded the men of the Golden Company to boil the flesh from his skull, [dip it in gold](wiki:Golden_skulls), and carry it before them when they crossed the [narrow sea](wiki:Narrow_sea) to retake [Westeros](wiki:Westeros). The [captains-general](wiki:Captain-general) who have since led the Golden Company have followed Aegor's example.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Radwd24.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_17-0)
65	
66	### Legacy
67	
68	According to a semi-canon source, Aegor had no children.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-18) The war cry of the [Golden Company](wiki:Golden_Company), "Beneath the gold, the bitter steel", pays homage to their founder.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Raffc21.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-1)
69	
70	## Appearances & Description
71	
72	Being half-[Targaryen](wiki:House_Targaryen), Aegor had [purple eyes](wiki:Purple_eyes), but black hair. As an adult, he had a close-cropped beard, little more than a shadow on his face. He looked like a warrior, tall and muscular, but was lean and lithe like a [panther](wiki:Panther).(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rssm1467.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-2)
73	
74	Aegor's armor was well-made but plain, grey-steel and black rings. On his helm was a [horsehead](wiki:Horse) crest, with a horse's mane flowing behind. His shield displayed his personal sigil: a stallion with black [dragon](wiki:Dragon) wings, on a golden field, snorting fire.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rssm1467.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-3)
75	
76	Angry all of his life, Aegor never smiled.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rssm1467.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-4) Reportedly, he was a hard man who had little use for anything beyond war.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aerys_i.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-0) Bittersteel was easily irritated and offended, sometimes attributed to his [Bracken](wiki:House_Bracken) blood.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_daeron_ii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-4) Aegor especially loathed his younger half-brother, [Brynden Rivers](wiki:Brynden_Rivers), and Brynden's mother, [Melissa Blackwood](wiki:Melissa_Blackwood), who had replaced Aegor's mother, [Barba Bracken](wiki:Barba_Bracken), as King [Aegon IV Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_IV_Targaryen)'s mistress.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rssm1467.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-5)
77	
78	## Narrative Arc
79	
80	### A Feast for Crows
81	
82	In [Dorne](wiki:Dorne), Princess [Arianne Martell](wiki:Arianne_Martell) tells Ser [Arys Oakheart](wiki:Arys_Oakheart) that the [Golden Company](wiki:Golden_Company) has broken their contract with [Myr](wiki:Myr), mentioning that the exiles are united by the dream of Bittersteel.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Raffc13.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-1)
83	
84	### A Dance with Dragons
85	
86	[Illyrio Mopatis](wiki:Illyrio_Mopatis) discusses the Golden Company with the exiled [Tyrion Lannister](wiki:Tyrion_Lannister), who recalls the story of Aegor's founding of the [sellsword](wiki:Sellsword) company. That night, Tyrion dreams of a battle in [Westeros](wiki:Westeros) in which he fights alongside Ser [Barristan Selmy](wiki:Barristan_Selmy) and Bittersteel with [dragons](wiki:Dragon) overhead. Tyrion kills his father, Lord [Tywin](wiki:Tywin_Lannister), and brother, Ser [Jaime](wiki:Jaime_Lannister), in the dream.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Radwd5.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-3) When Tyrion joins the [Second Sons](wiki:Second_Sons), [Tybero Istarion](wiki:Tybero_Istarion) informs him that Bittersteel had served with that company before establishing the Golden Company.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Radwd66.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-1)
87	
88	Aegor's [skull](wiki:Golden_skulls), along with those of successive [captains-general](wiki:Captain-general), is still in possession of the Golden Company. All are kept in the tent of the current captain-general, Ser [Harry Strickland](wiki:Harry_Strickland), with Aegor's displayed in the center on a tall pike. [Jon Connington](wiki:Jon_Connington) sees Aegor's grinning skull when he meets with the Golden Company in [Volon Therys](wiki:Volon_Therys).(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Radwd24.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_17-1) During the [landing of the Golden Company](wiki:Landing_of_the_Golden_Company) in the [stormlands](wiki:Stormlands), Jon realizes that Harry is a much more cautious leader than Aegor.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Radwd61.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_19-0)
89	
90	Barristan recalls how bloodshed resulted from Aegor and [Brynden Rivers](wiki:Brynden_Rivers) both having loved [Shiera Seastar](wiki:Shiera_Seastar).(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Radwd67.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_20-0)
91	
92	### The Winds of Winter
93	
94	At the [crofters' village](wiki:Crofters'_village), Ser [Justin Massey](wiki:Justin_Massey) advises [Stannis Baratheon](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon) to regroup across the [narrow sea](wiki:Narrow_sea) and raise a new army as Bittersteel did after the [Battle of the Redgrass Field](wiki:Battle_of_the_Redgrass_Field). Stannis refuses, however, as Aegor was a [bastard](wiki:Bastard) who never succeeded in putting any of [Daemon Blackfyre's](wiki:Daemon_I_Blackfyre) sons on the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne).(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-TheonI_21-0)
95	
96	## Quotes
97	
98	> Bitter his steel may have been, but worse was his tongue. He spilled [poison](wiki:Poison) in [Daemon](wiki:Daemon_I_Blackfyre)'s ear, and with him came the clamoring of other [knights](wiki:Knight) and [lords](wiki:Lord) with grievances.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_daeron_ii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-7)
99	>
100	> —writings of [Yandel](wiki:Yandel)
101	
102	> [The Mother](wiki:Mother_(the_Seven)) marked [Lord Rivers](wiki:Brynden_Rivers) on the day that he was born, and Bittersteel marked him once again upon the [Redgrass Field](wiki:Redgrass_Field).(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rtss.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-2)
103	>
104	> —[Sefton](wiki:Sefton_Staunton) to [Duncan the Tall](wiki:Duncan_the_Tall)
105	
106	> **Kyle**: Myself, I blame [Bloodraven](wiki:Bloodraven). He is the [King's Hand](wiki:Hand_of_the_King), yet he does nothing, whilst [the krakens](wiki:Ironborn) spread flame and terror up and down the [sunset sea](wiki:Sunset_sea).
107	>
108	> **Maynard**: His eye is fixed on [Tyrosh](wiki:Tyrosh), where Bittersteel sits in exile, plotting with the sons of [Daemon Blackfyre](wiki:Daemon_I_Blackfyre). So he keeps [the king](wiki:Aerys_I_Targaryen)'s ships close at hand, lest they attempt to cross.
109	>

## Identity

Aegor Rivers is a character.human from the AWOIAF wiki.

## Edges

- HOLDS_TITLE: Ser (track_b: Titles)
- HOLDS_TITLE: Captain-general (track_b: Titles)

- SWORN_TO: Golden Company (track_b: Allegiances)

- CULTURE_OF: Rivermen (track_b: Culture)
- BORN_AT: King's Landing (track_b: Born) [172 AC]
- DIED_AT: Disputed Lands (track_b: Died) [241 AC]
- PARENT_OF (reverse): Aegon IV Targaryen (track_b: Father)
- PARENT_OF (reverse): Barba Bracken (track_b: Mother)
- SPOUSE_OF: Calla Blackfyre (track_b: Spouse) [promised]

## Origins

### Early life

In [172 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest), Aegor was born at [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing) to the fifth of King [Aegon IV Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_IV_Targaryen)'s mistresses, [Barba Bracken](wiki:Barba_Bracken). Aegor was born only a fortnight before Aegon's wife, Queen [Naerys Targaryen](wiki:Naerys_Targaryen), nearly died in childbirth. With Naerys lingering near death, [Barba's father](wiki:Lord_Bracken_(Hand_of_the_King)), the [Hand of the King](wiki:Hand_of_the_King), began to speak openly of Barba marrying King Aegon IV. However, his words became a scandal when Queen Naerys recovered her health. Naerys's brother, Prince [Aemon the Dragonknight](wiki:Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_II)), and her son, Prince [Daeron Targaryen](wiki:Daeron_II_Targaryen), forced King Aegon to send both Barba and her [bastard](wiki:Bastard) son away from court. Aegor was raised at [Stone Hedge](wiki:Stone_Hedge) in the [riverlands](wiki:Riverlands) by the Brackens.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iv.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-2)

King Aegon IV was visiting Aegor at Stone Hedge in [177 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest) when he met Barba's younger sister, [Bethany Bracken](wiki:Bethany_Bracken), and took her for his seventh mistress. However, Bethany's affair with Ser [Terrence Toyne](wiki:Terrence_Toyne), a [Kingsguard](wiki:Kingsguard), soon resulted in the execution of Terrence, Bethany, and her father, Aegor's grandfather.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iv.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-3)

The angry Aegor reserved particular loathing for his half-brother [Brynden Rivers](wiki:Brynden_Rivers), whose mother, [Melissa Blackwood](wiki:Melissa_Blackwood), had replaced his own as their father's favorite mistress. Aegor loathed Brynden, who was known as Bloodraven, even further when [Shiera Seastar](wiki:Shiera_Seastar), their beautiful half-sister and a fellow [Great Bastard](wiki:Great_Bastard), chose Brynden over him. Their other half-brother, [Daemon Blackfyre](wiki:Daemon_I_Blackfyre), agreed to wed his daughter [Calla](wiki:Calla_Blackfyre) to Aegor; however, it is unknown if and when the marriage took place. Aegor often urged Daemon to press his own claim to the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne).(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_daeron_ii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-5)

### First Blackfyre Rebellion

In the [First Blackfyre Rebellion](wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion) in [196 AC](wiki:196_AC), Aegor sided with the pretender king [Daemon I Blackfyre](wiki:Daemon_I_Blackfyre) against King [Daeron II Targaryen](wiki:Daeron_II_Targaryen). During the [battle of the Redgrass Field](wiki:Battle_of_the_Redgrass_Field), Aegor commanded the right of Daemon's host.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rtmk.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-0)

To prevent a rout upon Daemon's death during the battle, Aegor picked up Daemon's [Valyrian steel](wiki:Valyrian_steel) sword, [Blackfyre](wiki:Blackfyre), rallied his troops, and madly charged into the [Raven's Teeth](wiki:Raven's_Teeth), the archers of Brynden Rivers. Bittersteel met Bloodraven in the middle of his charge, and a mighty duel followed. Though he took out Bloodraven's eye during their duel, Aegor ultimately fled the battlefield.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rtss.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-0)(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_daeron_ii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-6)

Bittersteel fled Westeros with Daemon's widow, [Rohanne of Tyrosh](wiki:Rohanne_of_Tyrosh), her surviving children, the blade Blackfyre, and hundreds of now-landless lords and knights, eventually settling in the [Free City](wiki:Free_Cities) of [Tyrosh](wiki:Tyrosh).(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rtss.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-1)(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Radwd5.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-0)

### The Golden Company

Aegor served a year with the [Second Sons](wiki:Second_Sons) mercenary company.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Radwd66.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-0) However, as other exile lords began to join other mercenary companies and the support for the [Blackfyres](wiki:House_Blackfyre) began to scatter, Bittersteel left the Second Sons to create the [Golden Company](wiki:Golden_Company), to bind the exiles together.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Radwd5.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-1) Shortly after its creation, the Golden Company became one of the most famous and disciplined [sellsword](wiki:Sellsword) companies known in the [Free Cities](wiki:Free_Cities). They quickly established their reputation by sacking [Qohor](wiki:Qohor) when the city refused to honor its contract.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rawoiafaegor_rivers.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-1) While sellswords are notoriously unreliable, the Golden Company has a respectable reputation. They are said to have never broken a contract.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Raffc13.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-0) The war cry of the Golden Company is "Beneath the [gold](wiki:Gold), the bitter [steel](wiki:Steel),"(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Raffc21.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-0)(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Radwd5.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-2) which refers to Aegor's nickname, Bittersteel.

The Golden Company was heavily involved in three of the four [Blackfyre Rebellions](wiki:Blackfyre_Pretenders) that followed the [first one](wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion). However, Aegor refused to support the pretender king [Daemon II Blackfyre](wiki:Daemon_II_Blackfyre) during the [Second Blackfyre Rebellion](wiki:Second_Blackfyre_Rebellion) in [211 AC](wiki:211_AC), and [maesters](wiki:Maester) of the [Citadel](wiki:Citadel) often debate why. Many believe that Daemon and Lord [Gormon Peake](wiki:Gormon_Peake) could not convince Aegor that their plan was sensible and could succeed despite the odds. Some think Bittersteel was a man who had little use for anything but war and distrusted Daemon's [dreams](wiki:Dream) and love of fine things, and some think Daemon's homosexuality troubled Aegor enough to deny him his assistance.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aerys_i.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-1) Bittersteel remained in [Essos](wiki:Essos) with the sword [Blackfyre](wiki:Blackfyre), a factor in the failure of the rebellion.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rtmk.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-1)

In [219 AC](wiki:219_AC), [Haegon I Blackfyre](wiki:Haegon_I_Blackfyre) and Bittersteel launched the [Third Blackfyre Rebellion](wiki:Third_Blackfyre_Rebellion). During the rebellion, Bloodraven and Bittersteel dueled for the second time. At the end of the battle, Haegon was killed after surrendering, but Aegor was captured and brought to the [Red Keep](wiki:Red_Keep) in chains. Though Brynden Rivers and Prince [Aerion Targaryen](wiki:Aerion_Targaryen) argued that Aegor should be executed, King [Aerys I Targaryen](wiki:Aerys_I_Targaryen) decided to send him to the [Wall](wiki:Wall) to join the [Night's Watch](wiki:Night's_Watch). However, the ship taking Aegor north was intercepted, and he was freed and returned to the Golden Company. Before the year was over, Aegor crowned [Daemon III Blackfyre](wiki:Daemon_III_Blackfyre), Haegon I's eldest son, and resumed plotting against King Aerys in [Tyrosh](wiki:Tyrosh).(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aerys_i.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-2)

During one of the Blackfyre Rebellions, Aegor swore a blood oath with [Torwyn Greyjoy](wiki:Torwyn_Greyjoy), but this [Lord Reaper of Pyke](wiki:Lord_Reaper_of_Pyke) betrayed Bittersteel to his enemies.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_iron_islands:_the_old_way_and_the_new.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_15-0)

In [236 AC](wiki:236_AC), Aegor landed on [Massey's Hook](wiki:Massey's_Hook) at the head of the Golden Company with Daemon III, starting the [Fourth Blackfyre Rebellion](wiki:Fourth_Blackfyre_Rebellion). The rebellion was crushed at the [Battle of Wendwater Bridge](wiki:Battle_of_Wendwater_Bridge), though Aegor managed to escape.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_v.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-1)(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_westerlands:_house_lannister_under_the_dragons.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_16-0) A few years after that battle, Aegor reappeared in the [Disputed Lands](wiki:Disputed_Lands), fighting with the Golden Company in a skirmish between Tyrosh and [Myr](wiki:Myr).(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_v.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-2)

When Aegor died at the age of sixty-nine in [241 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest), it is said he died as he had lived, with a sword in his hand and defiance upon his lips.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_v.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-3) On his deathbed, he commanded the men of the Golden Company to boil the flesh from his skull, [dip it in gold](wiki:Golden_skulls), and carry it before them when they crossed the [narrow sea](wiki:Narrow_sea) to retake [Westeros](wiki:Westeros). The [captains-general](wiki:Captain-general) who have since led the Golden Company have followed Aegor's example.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Radwd24.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_17-0)

### Legacy

According to a semi-canon source, Aegor had no children.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-18) The war cry of the [Golden Company](wiki:Golden_Company), "Beneath the gold, the bitter steel", pays homage to their founder.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Raffc21.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-1)

## Appearances & Description

Being half-[Targaryen](wiki:House_Targaryen), Aegor had [purple eyes](wiki:Purple_eyes), but black hair. As an adult, he had a close-cropped beard, little more than a shadow on his face. He looked like a warrior, tall and muscular, but was lean and lithe like a [panther](wiki:Panther).(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rssm1467.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-2)

Aegor's armor was well-made but plain, grey-steel and black rings. On his helm was a [horsehead](wiki:Horse) crest, with a horse's mane flowing behind. His shield displayed his personal sigil: a stallion with black [dragon](wiki:Dragon) wings, on a golden field, snorting fire.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rssm1467.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-3)

Angry all of his life, Aegor never smiled.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rssm1467.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-4) Reportedly, he was a hard man who had little use for anything beyond war.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aerys_i.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-0) Bittersteel was easily irritated and offended, sometimes attributed to his [Bracken](wiki:House_Bracken) blood.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_daeron_ii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-4) Aegor especially loathed his younger half-brother, [Brynden Rivers](wiki:Brynden_Rivers), and Brynden's mother, [Melissa Blackwood](wiki:Melissa_Blackwood), who had replaced Aegor's mother, [Barba Bracken](wiki:Barba_Bracken), as King [Aegon IV Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_IV_Targaryen)'s mistress.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rssm1467.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-5)

## Narrative Arc

### A Feast for Crows

In [Dorne](wiki:Dorne), Princess [Arianne Martell](wiki:Arianne_Martell) tells Ser [Arys Oakheart](wiki:Arys_Oakheart) that the [Golden Company](wiki:Golden_Company) has broken their contract with [Myr](wiki:Myr), mentioning that the exiles are united by the dream of Bittersteel.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Raffc13.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-1)

### A Dance with Dragons

[Illyrio Mopatis](wiki:Illyrio_Mopatis) discusses the Golden Company with the exiled [Tyrion Lannister](wiki:Tyrion_Lannister), who recalls the story of Aegor's founding of the [sellsword](wiki:Sellsword) company. That night, Tyrion dreams of a battle in [Westeros](wiki:Westeros) in which he fights alongside Ser [Barristan Selmy](wiki:Barristan_Selmy) and Bittersteel with [dragons](wiki:Dragon) overhead. Tyrion kills his father, Lord [Tywin](wiki:Tywin_Lannister), and brother, Ser [Jaime](wiki:Jaime_Lannister), in the dream.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Radwd5.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-3) When Tyrion joins the [Second Sons](wiki:Second_Sons), [Tybero Istarion](wiki:Tybero_Istarion) informs him that Bittersteel had served with that company before establishing the Golden Company.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Radwd66.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-1)

Aegor's [skull](wiki:Golden_skulls), along with those of successive [captains-general](wiki:Captain-general), is still in possession of the Golden Company. All are kept in the tent of the current captain-general, Ser [Harry Strickland](wiki:Harry_Strickland), with Aegor's displayed in the center on a tall pike. [Jon Connington](wiki:Jon_Connington) sees Aegor's grinning skull when he meets with the Golden Company in [Volon Therys](wiki:Volon_Therys).(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Radwd24.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_17-1) During the [landing of the Golden Company](wiki:Landing_of_the_Golden_Company) in the [stormlands](wiki:Stormlands), Jon realizes that Harry is a much more cautious leader than Aegor.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Radwd61.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_19-0)

Barristan recalls how bloodshed resulted from Aegor and [Brynden Rivers](wiki:Brynden_Rivers) both having loved [Shiera Seastar](wiki:Shiera_Seastar).(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Radwd67.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_20-0)

### The Winds of Winter

At the [crofters' village](wiki:Crofters'_village), Ser [Justin Massey](wiki:Justin_Massey) advises [Stannis Baratheon](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon) to regroup across the [narrow sea](wiki:Narrow_sea) and raise a new army as Bittersteel did after the [Battle of the Redgrass Field](wiki:Battle_of_the_Redgrass_Field). Stannis refuses, however, as Aegor was a [bastard](wiki:Bastard) who never succeeded in putting any of [Daemon Blackfyre's](wiki:Daemon_I_Blackfyre) sons on the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne).(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-TheonI_21-0)

## Quotes

> Bitter his steel may have been, but worse was his tongue. He spilled [poison](wiki:Poison) in [Daemon](wiki:Daemon_I_Blackfyre)'s ear, and with him came the clamoring of other [knights](wiki:Knight) and [lords](wiki:Lord) with grievances.(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_daeron_ii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-7)
>
> —writings of [Yandel](wiki:Yandel)

> [The Mother](wiki:Mother_(the_Seven)) marked [Lord Rivers](wiki:Brynden_Rivers) on the day that he was born, and Bittersteel marked him once again upon the [Redgrass Field](wiki:Redgrass_Field).(wiki:Aegor_Rivers.cite_ref-Rtss.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-2)
>
> —[Sefton](wiki:Sefton_Staunton) to [Duncan the Tall](wiki:Duncan_the_Tall)

> **Kyle**: Myself, I blame [Bloodraven](wiki:Bloodraven). He is the [King's Hand](wiki:Hand_of_the_King), yet he does nothing, whilst [the krakens](wiki:Ironborn) spread flame and terror up and down the [sunset sea](wiki:Sunset_sea).
>
> **Maynard**: His eye is fixed on [Tyrosh](wiki:Tyrosh), where Bittersteel sits in exile, plotting with the sons of [Daemon Blackfyre](wiki:Daemon_I_Blackfyre). So he keeps [the king](wiki:Aerys_I_Targaryen)'s ships close at hand, lest they attempt to cross.
>
> —[Kyle the Cat](wiki:Kyle_the_Cat) and [Maynard Plumm](wiki:Maynard_Plumm)

> **Tommard**: ...beggar's feast you've laid before us. Without Bittersteel... **Gormon**: Bittersteel be buggered. No [bastard](wiki:Bastard) can be trusted, not even him. 29	In [161 AC](wiki:161_AC), King [Baelor I Targaryen](wiki:Baelor_I_Targaryen) imprisoned his sisters — the Princesses [Daena](wiki:Daena_Targaryen), [Rhaena](wiki:Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aegon_III)), and [Elaena Targaryen](wiki:Elaena_Targaryen) — within the [Maidenvault](wiki:Maidenvault) in the [Red Keep](wiki:Red_Keep). The three princesses were kept in comfortable confinement, and to gain favor with Baelor, [lords](wiki:Lord) and [knights](wiki:Knight) sent their [maiden](wiki:Maiden) relatives to the Maidenvault to be their [lady companions](wiki:Lady_companion).(wiki:Barba_Bracken.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_baelor_i.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0) Near the end of Baelor's reign, one of these maidens was Barba Bracken, daughter to [Lord Bracken](wiki:Lord_Bracken_(Hand_of_the_King)) of [Stone Hedge](wiki:Stone_Hedge). When King Baelor I died in [171 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest), King [Viserys II Targaryen](wiki:Viserys_II_Targaryen) released the princesses from their confinement, and sixteen-year-old Barba met Prince [Aegon Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_IV_Targaryen), who became entranced with her.(wiki:Barba_Bracken.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iv.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-3)
30

Barba gave birth to a [bastard](wiki:Bastard) son of Aegon, [Aegor Rivers](wiki:Aegor_Rivers), only a fortnight before Queen [Naerys Targaryen](wiki:Naerys_Targaryen) nearly died in childbirth. With Naerys lingering near death, Barba's father began to speak openly of Barba marrying King Aegon IV. However, Queen Naerys recovered, and the talk about Barba marrying the king became a scandal. Princes [Aemon](wiki:Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_II)) and [Daeron Targaryen](wiki:Daeron_II_Targaryen), Naerys's brother and son, forced King Aegon to send both Barba and her bastard son away from court. They returned to Stone Hedge, where Barba raised her son Aegor.(wiki:Barba_Bracken.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iv.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-4)

33	After Barba, King Aegon IV took [Melissa Blackwood](wiki:Melissa_Blackwood) as his mistress, who was younger, prettier, and more modest. In response, Barba and her father began grooming Barba's younger sister, [Bethany](wiki:Bethany_Bracken), to replace Melissa. This eventually worked in [177 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest), when Bethany caught Aegon's eye while he was visiting Stone Hedge to see Aegor. However, in [178 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest), Bethany was caught abed with the handsome knight of the [Kingsguard](wiki:Kingsguard), Ser [Terrence Toyne](wiki:Terrence_Toyne), and both Bethany and Lord Bracken were executed.(wiki:Barba_Bracken.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iv.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-5)

After Barba was sent away from court, Aegon took Melissa "Missy" Blackwood as his mistress; she was slender, and Barba was heard to say that she was flat as a boy. When Aegon heard how Barba had disparaged his mistress, he renamed the hills Missy's Teats,(wiki:Barba_Bracken.cite_ref-Radwd48.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-2) and granted them to [House Blackwood](wiki:House_Blackwood).(wiki:Barba_Bracken.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iv.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-6) The Blackwoods still call the hills Missy's Teats, and the Brackens still call them Barba's Teats.(wiki:Barba_Bracken.cite_ref-Radwd48.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-3)

## Origins

In [161 AC](wiki:161_AC), King [Baelor I Targaryen](wiki:Baelor_I_Targaryen) imprisoned his sisters — the Princesses [Daena](wiki:Daena_Targaryen), [Rhaena](wiki:Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aegon_III)), and [Elaena Targaryen](wiki:Elaena_Targaryen) — within the [Maidenvault](wiki:Maidenvault) in the [Red Keep](wiki:Red_Keep). The three princesses were kept in comfortable confinement, and to gain favor with Baelor, [lords](wiki:Lord) and [knights](wiki:Knight) sent their [maiden](wiki:Maiden) relatives to the Maidenvault to be their [lady companions](wiki:Lady_companion).(wiki:Barba_Bracken.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_baelor_i.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0) Near the end of Baelor's reign, one of these maidens was Barba Bracken, daughter to [Lord Bracken](wiki:Lord_Bracken_(Hand_of_the_King)) of [Stone Hedge](wiki:Stone_Hedge). When King Baelor I died in [171 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest), King [Viserys II Targaryen](wiki:Viserys_II_Targaryen) released the princesses from their confinement, and sixteen-year-old Barba met Prince [Aegon Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_IV_Targaryen), who became entranced with her.(wiki:Barba_Bracken.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iv.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-3)

When King Viserys II died in [172 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest), Prince Aegon ascended the throne as King Aegon IV Targaryen, named Barba's father his [Hand of the King](wiki:Hand_of_the_King), and openly took Barba as his [mistress](wiki:Mistress). Barba gave birth to a [bastard](wiki:Bastard) son of Aegon, [Aegor Rivers](wiki:Aegor_Rivers), only a fortnight before Queen [Naerys Targaryen](wiki:Naerys_Targaryen) nearly died in childbirth. With Naerys lingering near death, Barba's father began to speak openly of Barba marrying King Aegon IV. However, Queen Naerys recovered, and the talk about Barba marrying the king became a scandal. Princes [Aemon](wiki:Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_II)) and [Daeron Targaryen](wiki:Daeron_II_Targaryen), Naerys's brother and son, forced King Aegon to send both Barba and her bastard son away from court. They returned to Stone Hedge, where Barba raised her son Aegor.(wiki:Barba_Bracken.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iv.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-4)

After Barba, King Aegon IV took [Melissa Blackwood](wiki:Melissa_Blackwood) as his mistress, who was younger, prettier, and more modest. In response, Barba and her father began grooming Barba's younger sister, [Bethany](wiki:Bethany_Bracken), to replace Melissa. This eventually worked in [177 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest), when Bethany caught Aegon's eye while he was visiting Stone Hedge to see Aegor. However, in [178 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest), Bethany was caught abed with the handsome knight of the [Kingsguard](wiki:Kingsguard), Ser [Terrence Toyne](wiki:Terrence_Toyne), and both Bethany and Lord Bracken were executed.(wiki:Barba_Bracken.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iv.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-5)

### Barba's Teats

In [172 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest), King Aegon IV Targaryen was visiting Stone Hedge when he saw the hills called the [Mother's Teats](wiki:Teats), and renamed them Barba's Teats on account of their resemblance to his buxom mistress's breasts. After Barba was sent away from court, Aegon took Melissa "Missy" Blackwood as his mistress; she was slender, and Barba was heard to say that she was flat as a boy. When Aegon heard how Barba had disparaged his mistress, he renamed the hills Missy's Teats,(wiki:Barba_Bracken.cite_ref-Radwd48.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-2) and granted them to [House Blackwood](wiki:House_Blackwood).(wiki:Barba_Bracken.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iv.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-6) The Blackwoods still call the hills Missy's Teats, and the Brackens still call them Barba's Teats.(wiki:Barba_Bracken.cite_ref-Radwd48.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-3)

## Appearances & Description

Barba was buxom(wiki:Barba_Bracken.cite_ref-Radwd48.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-0) and had dark hair.(wiki:Barba_Bracken.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iv.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0) She was vivacious, and not very modest.(wiki:Barba_Bracken.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iv.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-1) The [Blackwoods](wiki:House_Blackwood) claim Barba was not as beautiful as [Melissa Blackwood](wiki:Melissa_Blackwood),(wiki:Barba_Bracken.cite_ref-Radwd48.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-1) and [Maester](wiki:Maester) [Yandel](wiki:Yandel) also describes Melissa as prettier than Barba.(wiki:Barba_Bracken.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iv.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-2)
